task_completion.description: MRHead volume is loaded and visible in 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 3D Slicer interface is visible with the MRHead brain MRI loaded. The brain should be visible in the slice viewers, particularly the Red viewer.

task_completion.description: The output GIF file is saved at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal shows the file 'brain_axial_sweep.gif' located exactly in '/home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/'.

task_completion.description: The output file is a valid GIF with sufficient size.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File properties, a thumbnail, or an image viewer confirms the file is a GIF and has a file size greater than a few kilobytes, indicating it contains actual image data.

task_completion.description: The GIF displays the MRHead brain MRI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The opened GIF or its thumbnail clearly shows an axial slice of a brain MRI (MRHead).

task_completion.description: The GIF contains an animation sweeping through at least 30 axial slices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIF is shown playing, revealing a sweep through different axial slices of the brain, or file properties/Screen Capture settings confirm at least 30 frames were captured.

integrity.description: Agent used 3D Slicer to generate the GIF.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with 3D Slicer's Screen Capture module or python console to create the animation, rather than downloading a pre-made file.

integrity.description: The GIF was created during the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file creation time or modification time in the file manager is recent, matching the time of the task execution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The MRHead dataset is a standard 3D Slicer sample MRI scan containing 130 axial slices. In 3D Slicer, the 'Red' viewer is the default window for displaying Axial slices. The expected view for the axial sweep is the Red viewer.
